message:
feat(theme): add NO_COLOR standard support for accessibility (ntm-952.5.2)

Implement the NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/) to support users
who need maximum legibility or are piping output.

Changes:
- Add Plain theme variant with empty colors (uses terminal defaults)
- Add NoColorEnabled() function that respects:
  - Standard NO_COLOR environment variable (any value = disabled)
  - NTM_NO_COLOR for NTM-specific override (0=force colors, 1=no colors)
- Update FromName() to check NoColorEnabled() first
- Add "plain", "none", "no-color", "nocolor" as explicit theme names

Usage:
- Set NO_COLOR=1 to disable all colors (follows standard)
- Set NTM_NO_COLOR=0 to force colors ON (overrides NO_COLOR)
- Set NTM_THEME=plain for explicit no-color theme

Tests added for all NO_COLOR detection scenarios.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-952.5","title":"SUB-EPIC: Accessibility & Terminal Compatibility","description":"Focus: accessibility and terminal compatibility.

Deliverables:
- Reduced-motion mode (no shimmer/pulse)
- Safer icon and width fallbacks (no broken gutters)
- Better contrast defaults and an escape hatch for low-color terminals","design":"## Terminal constraints
We can’t rely on:
- Nerd Fonts
- good color support
- wide panes
- low-latency refresh

So accessibility is largely about **fallbacks** and **stability**.

## Reduced motion
Make it easy to disable shimmer/pulse (including for CI snapshots).

## No-color
Honor NO_COLOR and avoid relying on color alone to convey meaning.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.84566752-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.955632272-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

 {"id":"ntm-952.6","title":"SUB-EPIC: Performance & Responsiveness","description":"Focus: responsiveness and reliability.

Deliverables:
- Refresh budgets and caching (avoid expensive tmux capture loops)
- Graceful degradation when tmux data is missing/slow
- Debug affordances for diagnosing “why is this slow?”","design":"## Performance philosophy
Do less work by default:
- cache tmux captures
- coalesce refreshes
- cap line budgets

## Measure
When possible, surface timing/latency in an opt-in debug view. It’s hard to polish what you can’t observe.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.871798196-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.986947614-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

@@ -226,9 +273,36 @@ var Nord = Theme{
 // Default is the currently active theme
 var Default = CatppuccinMocha
 
+// NoColorEnabled returns true if color output should be disabled.
+// Respects the NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/):
+// - If NO_COLOR exists in environment (any value), colors are disabled
+// - NTM_NO_COLOR=1 also disables colors
+// - NTM_NO_COLOR=0 forces colors ON (overrides NO_COLOR)
+func NoColorEnabled() bool {
+	// NTM-specific override takes precedence
+	ntmNoColor := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NTM_NO_COLOR"))
+	switch strings.ToLower(ntmNoColor) {
+	case "0", "false", "no", "off":
+		return false // Force colors on
+	case "1", "true", "yes", "on":
+		return true // Force colors off
+	}
+
+	// Check standard NO_COLOR (presence means disabled, regardless of value)
+	_, noColorSet := os.LookupEnv("NO_COLOR")
+	return noColorSet
+}
+
 // FromName returns a theme by name
 func FromName(name string) Theme {
+	// Always return Plain theme if NO_COLOR is enabled
+	if NoColorEnabled() {
+		return Plain
+	}
+
 	switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name)) {
+	case "plain", "none", "no-color", "nocolor":
+		return Plain
 	case "macchiato":
 		return CatppuccinMacchiato
